cards.name: Join Zsolt Jozsef Simon — Porcelain Casting Workshop at Art & Design Building
cards.blurb: Grab something sweet at Swana Sweets while you're out on the Student Union patio and soak in the mild morning air.
cards.why: Perfect way to kick off a campus-centered day.

cards.name: Introduction to Python and Pandas at King Library
cards.blurb: Drop into this free Python and Pandas intro at King Library. No laptop? Just show up and see what it's about.
cards.why: Free, practical, and you'll actually walk away knowing something new.

cards.name: R&R, 2026: Recovering Ricardo Favela’s Royal Chicano Air Force Poster Collection at San José State University at King Library
cards.blurb: Browse the R&R, 2026 exhibition at King Library, a showcase of recovered posters from Ricardo Favela's Royal Chicano Air Force collection. Local history with serious visual punch.
cards.why: It's free art with real cultural weight right there on campus.

cards.name: Be Ready: Be Prepared for Disasters
cards.blurb: Swing by this disaster preparedness talk and actually get some useful info on what to do when things go sideways in the South Bay.
cards.why: Free and genuinely useful knowledge you'll be glad you have.

cards.name: Natalie and James Thompson Gallery: Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at ART 127: Thompson Gallery
cards.blurb: Check out Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at the Thompson Gallery. It's a thought-provoking show exploring how tech shapes the way we communicate.
cards.why: It's the kind of free art show that actually gives you something to talk about over dinner.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide
cards.address: 588 E Alma Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head to the ballpark for San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide. Get there early for decent seats, grab a beer and some ballpark food, and settle in for nine innings under the lights.
cards.why: A Thursday night baseball game on a 72-degree day is hard to beat.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.address: 62 S Second St
cards.blurb: If the game wraps up early or you're in the mood to keep the night going, Anthony Jeselnik is performing at 62 S Second St tonight. Dark humor, sharp delivery, zero chill.
cards.why: He's one of the best working comics right now and he's right here tonight.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman talk about embracing limits and doing less — it's a surprisingly refreshing way to kick off a Thursday morning.
cards.why: Free, thoughtful, and a genuinely good excuse to be out early.

cards.name: Market Street Spring Jubilee
cards.blurb: Hit the Market Street Spring Jubilee for live music and the general vibe of a street fest on a warm afternoon.
cards.why: 70 degrees and live music outside is pretty much the ideal Thursday.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Wander through Jessica Monette's project room exhibition at SCU — soil, roots, and becoming, all in one quiet gallery space.
cards.why: It's free art you can actually stand in front of and think about.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Drop into this free session on AI-powered search tools — practical, fast, and you'll probably leave knowing something useful.
cards.why: It's the kind of low-key skill upgrade that actually sticks.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.address: 62 S Second St
cards.blurb: Anthony Jeselnik live at 62 S Second St in San Jose. Dark, sharp comedy that does not pull punches.
cards.why: He's genuinely one of the best working stand-ups right now.

cards.name: Ethics Certificate Training for Students: April 9 & 16
cards.blurb: Sit in on the Ethics Certificate Training at SCU — even if you're not a student, the conversations in these sessions tend to be worth it.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a good way to fill the early evening.

cards.name: Chicago Presidential Reception
cards.blurb: Swing by the Chicago Presidential Reception at SCU — free event, good energy, and a solid reason to put on real clothes.
cards.why: You don't need a reason beyond free food and interesting people.

cards.name: Vibe & Thrive Mini Health Fair at Science Lawn
cards.blurb: Check out the Vibe & Thrive Mini Health Fair on the Science Lawn — early bird hours mean shorter lines and a chill vibe to ease into the morning.
cards.why: It's happening right now and it's free, so why not kick off the day with something different.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman's Spark60 talk on embracing your limits. It's a lecture-style format, so grab a seat and actually sit with the ideas he's throwing out.
cards.why: A talk about doing less and living better hits different first thing in the morning.

cards.name: Market Street Spring Jubilee
cards.blurb: Head to the Market Street Spring Jubilee for live music and the general good energy of a street festival on a 72-degree afternoon.
cards.why: Live music outside in perfect weather is hard to beat on a Thursday.

cards.name: Jessica Monette: Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming (Project Room Exhibition)
cards.blurb: Walk through Jessica Monette's Project Room Exhibition, Root Me in the Soil, In the Wake and the Becoming. Take your time with it; the work rewards slow looking.
cards.why: Free art that's actually worth the stop, not just filler.

cards.name: Be Ready: Be Prepared for Disasters
cards.blurb: Pop into the Be Ready: Be Prepared for Disasters community session. It's practical, genuinely useful info, and worth having if you live in the Bay Area.
cards.why: You've been meaning to actually learn this stuff, and now there's no excuse.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.address: 62 S Second St
cards.blurb: Anthony Jeselnik live at 62 S Second St. He's sharp, dark, and doesn't hold back, so go in knowing exactly what you're signing up for.
cards.why: Stand-up comedy on a Thursday night is the right call.

cards.name: GIS & Drone Society Club Meeting
cards.blurb: Swing by the GIS & Drone Society Club Meeting if you're at all curious about mapping tech or drones. It's low-key and open.
cards.why: Good way to fill the early evening with something genuinely nerdy and fun.

cards.name: Homework Help for Grades K-6
cards.blurb: Join the Qigong class at Cupertino Library — a low-key, grounding way to ease into the day with some gentle movement.
cards.why: Starting the morning with intentional movement sets a great tone for the rest of the day.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Main Street Cupertino and grab lunch at Lazy Dog or one of the Asian spots along the plaza — walk the outdoor space after and enjoy the afternoon air.
cards.why: It's right in Cupertino and the patio vibes are perfect for a mild April afternoon.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Catch this free talk at Santa Clara University on AI-powered search tools — practical, interesting, and worth knowing about even if you're not a tech person.
cards.why: Free, genuinely useful info and a good way to spend an hour and a half after lunch.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Triton Museum of Art — check out the contemporary galleries and spend some time in the sculpture garden before they close.
cards.why: Free world-class art that most people in the South Bay have never been to. Worth it every time.

cards.name: Bakenstein
cards.address: 1370 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Bakenstein for something sweet before the evening winds up — rated 4.9 and worth every bite.
cards.why: A pre-meditation treat from one of the highest-rated spots in Santa Clara is a solid call.

cards.name: Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness
cards.blurb: End the day at Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness in Cupertino. Drop in, decompress, and let the week melt off.
cards.why: Bookending a Qigong morning with an evening meditation session is a genuinely great Thursday.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Join the Spotlight Tour at the Anderson Collection and let a docent walk you through the highlights of one of the best collections of postwar American art in the country.
cards.why: Free, no tickets needed, and a great warm-up before you come back to draw later.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch this new performance piece by Erika Chong Shuch at the Bing Studio — it's the kind of intimate, experimental work that's worth seeing without knowing too much going in.
cards.why: Free and it's happening right now on campus, so just go.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Head to the Graphic Novel Book Club. Even if you didn't read the book, these things are usually pretty welcoming and the conversation gets good fast.
cards.why: A free, low-key social hour that breaks up the visual art heavy evening nicely.

cards.name: Friends of The Library Membership Month Book Sale - Friends Preview Sale (w/ Annual Meeting)
cards.blurb: Browse the Friends Preview Sale before it opens to the general public — this is the good shift where you actually find stuff worth buying.
cards.why: Free books, good crowd, and you get first pick.

cards.name: Bloomsgiving
cards.address: 301 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner here on Castro St — it's the kind of spot that feels right for a Thursday evening when you want something good without a whole production.
cards.why: Solid food, chill vibe, and they close at 8 so you're getting in at the right time.

cards.name: Pelicans, Herons, and Egrets, Oh My!
cards.blurb: Head to this free talk about pelicans, herons, and egrets in Los Altos. Whether you're a birder or just Bay-Area-curious, it's a genuinely interesting way to spend an evening.
cards.why: Free, educational, and way more fun than it sounds.

cards.name: Toddler Time
cards.blurb: Catch Read Me, the arts event in Mountain View. Show up, take it in, and see what's on the walls or stage tonight.
cards.why: It's a locked item for a reason — local arts nights like this are worth the effort.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: 317 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a quick stop at Books Inc. on Castro. Browse whatever's on the staff picks table and grab something to read this weekend.
cards.why: Open until 10 and it's the perfect low-key cap to the night.

cards.name: Drop-In Drawing @ The Anderson
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Drop in and draw at the Anderson Collection — no prep needed, just show up and sketch in one of Stanford's best art spaces.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a genuinely nice way to spend a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Rodin sculpture garden, then head inside to browse the permanent collection and whatever's currently on view. It's free and genuinely world-class.
cards.why: You're already on campus and this is one of the best free museums in the Bay Area.

cards.name: Lou Henry Hoover Carillon Performance
cards.address: 550 Jane Stanford Way, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Catch the Lou Henry Hoover Carillon performance on campus. It's a short stop but the bells filling the Stanford air is a pretty surreal midday moment.
cards.why: It's happening today and it costs almost nothing to swing by.

cards.name: Spotlight Tours Thursdays | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Join the Thursday Spotlight Tour at the Anderson Collection and get a guided look at the pieces you just walked past on your own.
cards.why: Having a docent break down the collection makes it stick in a way solo browsing doesn't.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Head up the Dish Trail for the late afternoon loop. The light at this hour is great and you'll get those wide foothill views looking out over the Bay.
cards.why: 72°F and sunny is basically the ideal Dish weather.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at the Graphic Novel Book Club. Even if you haven't read this month's pick, it's the kind of crowd where that's fine.
cards.why: Good conversation with people who take comics seriously is a solid Thursday night.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit up a handful of the vendors, grab a coffee to start, then settle into tacos or banh mi on the patio before the lunch rush gets real.
cards.why: Best way to ease into the day with zero commitment and great food.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk the grounds and take in the spring blooms — April is peak season and the garden looks genuinely stunning right now.
cards.why: It's free, it's beautiful, and 72°F is perfect for a slow stroll.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Work through the contemporary galleries at your own pace, then spend some time out in the sculpture garden before they close.
cards.why: Free world-class art with a sculpture garden is hard to beat on a Thursday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the boutiques along Santana Row, then grab a table at Straits or the Zinnia patio for dinner before the evening kicks off.
cards.why: It's the kind of place that feels effortlessly good on a mild spring evening.

cards.name: Anthony Jeselnik
cards.address: 62 S Second St
cards.blurb: Anthony Jeselnik is one of the sharpest, darkest stand-up comics working right now. Show up, sit back, and let it rip.
cards.why: Free stand-up from a headliner-level comic is a genuinely rare Thursday night.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Catch Oliver Burkeman's talk on embracing limits and getting more done by doing less — it's a genuinely interesting angle on productivity and mortality that'll have you thinking all day.
cards.why: Free talk, sharp ideas, and a good way to kick off a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Triton's current contemporary exhibitions and spend some time in the sculpture garden outside. It's genuinely world-class work in a low-key setting that never feels crowded.
cards.why: Free admission and you're already in Santa Clara, so it's a natural next stop.

cards.name: Hungry Hound
cards.address: 1000 Lafayette St C, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Hungry Hound before the afternoon fills up. It's a straightforward, well-rated spot with prices that won't hurt.
cards.why: Under $15 and rated 4.5 — hard to beat for a quick, solid lunch.

cards.name: AI-Powered tools to help with your searching
cards.blurb: Sit in on this free session on AI-powered search tools. Whether you use it for work or personal research, you'll walk out with at least a couple of tricks you didn't have before.
cards.why: It's free, practical, and you'll actually use what you learn.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Take a slow walk through the rose garden while the afternoon light is still good. April is prime blooming season so the color and fragrance are at their peak right now.
cards.why: Perfect spring afternoon for it, and it costs nothing.

cards.name: Palermo Italian Restaurant
cards.address: 791 Auzerais Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Finish the night with dinner at Palermo. Order whatever the kitchen is pushing and lean into the Italian comfort food in a low-key San Jose neighborhood spot.
cards.why: Solid 4.5 rating, reasonable prices, and a relaxed vibe to close out the day.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the galleries and spend real time in the sculpture garden outside. The contemporary art here holds up and the free admission makes it a no-brainer.
cards.why: You're already in the arts headspace from Burkeman's talk, so keep that energy going somewhere beautiful.

cards.name: Taplands
cards.address: 1171 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a seat at Taplands and pick through their rotating tap list over lunch. Great spot to decompress with a pint after a full morning.
cards.why: It's a solid local spot in Santa Clara and a perfect midday reset.

cards.name: Natalie and James Thompson Gallery: Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at ART 127: Thompson Gallery
cards.blurb: Check out the Click on My Mouth exhibit at Thompson Gallery, where the show digs into the intersection of language and technology through contemporary art.
cards.why: It ties right into the themes from this morning and it's free to walk in.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 118 Paseo de San Antonio, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Stop at Philz for an afternoon cup before the evening kicks off. Good call on a day this full.
cards.why: You'll want the recharge before dinner and it closes at 8 so the timing works perfectly.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for dinner and graze through the vendor stalls. Mix and match from tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good, and grab a patio seat if the evening warms up.
cards.why: No better way to end a full day than grazing through a dozen options with no pressure to commit to one thing.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek trail and let the morning shake off. It's shaded, flat, and genuinely pretty along the water.
cards.why: 54°F now and climbing to 74°F makes this the sweet spot for a morning walk.

cards.name: Trattoria 360
cards.address: 360 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through Vasona Lake on your way out, grab a bench by the water, and decompress before lunch.
cards.why: It's a genuinely relaxing spot on a clear spring day.

cards.name: Recycle Bookstore West
cards.address: 275 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Recycle Bookstore West on Campbell Ave. Used books, good prices, and the kind of place you lose an hour in without noticing.
cards.why: Great way to wind down after a walk without committing to anything intense.

cards.name: Bayon Temple
cards.address: 2425 S Bascom Ave suite a, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Bayon Temple. It's a Cambodian spot on Bascom with a strong local following. Try the local favorite dishes and take your time with it.
cards.why: Solid 4.6 rating and a cuisine you don't see everywhere.

cards.name: Craft Club for Adults: The Joy of Drawing - Tulips in Spring
cards.blurb: Head to the Saratoga library for the drop-in drawing class focused on tulips. No experience needed, just show up and draw.
cards.why: Free, creative, and a nice afternoon change of pace.

cards.name: Body Rest Massage Wellness Center
cards.address: 621 E Campbell Ave #11e, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Body Rest Massage Wellness Center on Campbell Ave. Let someone else take care of you for an hour after a full morning out.
cards.why: 4.8 rating and open until 9 PM, so you've got plenty of flexibility.

cards.name: Forbes Mill Steakhouse
cards.address: 206 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Forbes Mill Steakhouse on North Santa Cruz. This is a proper splurge night. Go with the steak and don't skip dessert.
cards.why: It's a Thursday night and you deserve a real dinner out.

cards.name: Horizon Bakery
cards.address: 19929 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Horizon Bakery for lunch. Grab a pastry, a savory bite, and something to sip while you settle in at a table.
cards.why: It's right in Cupertino and rated 4.6 for a reason — solid spot to refuel after qigong.

cards.name: Stevens Canyon Trail
cards.address: 13492 Stevens Canyon Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Hit Stevens Canyon Trail for a few hours of foothill hiking. The trail runs along the creek with good tree cover and enough elevation to feel like you actually went somewhere.
cards.why: 72°F and sunny is basically perfect hiking weather for this time of year.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wander Main Street Cupertino and browse the outdoor plaza. Good for window shopping, people-watching, and grabbing a coffee or snack before the evening.
cards.why: The outdoor vibe is easy and relaxed, and there's plenty to poke around without any pressure.

cards.name: Astounding Massage Management
cards.address: 4155 Moorpark Ave, San Jose, CA 95117, USA
cards.blurb: Book a slot at Astounding Massage Management in San Jose for a proper evening massage. Rated 4.9, so expectations are high and apparently met.
cards.why: After a hiking day, this is exactly what your legs are asking for.

cards.name: Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Cap the night at the Thursday Evening Meditation and Mindfulness session at Cupertino Library. Show up, breathe, decompress.
cards.why: Free, mellow, and a genuinely good way to close out a Thursday.

cards.name: Hummus Mediterranean Kitchen
cards.address: 715 Stanford Shopping Center, Palo Alto, CA 94304, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the evening with a solid Mediterranean spread at Hummus Mediterranean Kitchen inside Stanford Shopping Center.
cards.why: It's a relaxed, satisfying dinner before the night picks up.

cards.name: Color Me Mine
cards.address: 321 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into Color Me Mine on University Ave and browse the studio while you wait to head to your next stop.
cards.why: Gets you in a creative headspace right before the drawing session.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Grab a seat at the Graphic Novel Book Club and dig into whatever book is on the table tonight.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a fun way to meet other people who actually read.

cards.name: Sundance The Steakhouse
cards.address: 1921 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the night at Sundance The Steakhouse with a proper late dinner, think a good cut and a glass of something worth savoring.
cards.why: It's a classic Palo Alto spot that earns its reputation every time.

cards.name: SaiGon BreadFast
cards.address: 123 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Hit SaiGon BreadFast early for a banh mi and some coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: 4.6 stars and a 7 PM close means it works perfectly for a morning start.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum working through the exhibits on how we got from punch cards to pocket supercomputers.
cards.why: It's genuinely fascinating stuff even if you're not a tech person, and $20 gets you a solid two hours.

cards.name: La Avenida Bike Trail Head
cards.address: 1003-1001 La Avenida St, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Take the La Avenida Bike Trail for a midday stretch along the bay path, great for a walk or a ride if you brought wheels.
cards.why: 71 degrees and 5% rain chance is basically perfect trail weather.

cards.name: Bloomsgiving
cards.address: 301 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Bloomsgiving on Castro St, a solid local spot to refuel before the afternoon.
cards.why: 4.6 stars and open until 8 PM, it fits cleanly into the early afternoon slot.

cards.name: Next Level Board Game Cafe
cards.address: 888 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at Next Level Board Game Cafe. Grab dinner, pick a game from their library, and settle in for a few rounds.
cards.why: 4.9 rating and open until 10 PM makes it the best possible Thursday night closer.

cards.name: Izzy's Brooklyn Bagels
cards.address: 477 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a bagel and coffee at this California Ave staple — order a classic everything bagel with lox and settle in.
cards.why: It's a proper breakfast spot and closes at 3 PM so morning is when it shines.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit the Stanford Ave trailhead and take the loop up to The Dish — the views of the Bay from the top are worth the climb, and the 72°F high means it'll be comfortable by the time you're heading back down.
cards.why: Perfect morning weather for this iconic hike before the day heats up.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Cool off at the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. Walk the Rodin sculpture garden outside, then head in for a spin through the galleries — free and genuinely world-class.
cards.why: It's free, it's on campus, and it's a great way to shift gears after the hike.

cards.name: Evvia Estiatorio
cards.address: 420 Emerson St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Evvia for a late Greek lunch. Order the lamb chops or whatever the mezze spread looks like today — this is a proper sit-down meal worth lingering over.
cards.why: One of the best restaurants in Palo Alto and worth every dollar at the $30-60 range.

cards.name: Drop-In Drawing @ The Anderson
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Drop into the Anderson Collection for free drawing time — bring your own sketchbook or just show up and work from the art around you.
cards.why: It's a free, low-key creative afternoon and a great way to spend a couple hours at Stanford.

cards.name: Watercourse Way Bath House Spa
cards.address: 165 Channing Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Book a private soaking tub at Watercourse Way and decompress for an hour or two. It's a quintessential Palo Alto move and the perfect way to wind down your body after the hike.
cards.why: Open until 11 PM and genuinely restorative after an active day.

cards.name: Graphic Novel Book Club
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at the Graphic Novel Book Club. Even if you haven't read whatever they're discussing, these things are usually a great crowd and a fun conversation.
cards.why: It's free, it's tonight, and it's a nice social cap to a full day.

cards.name: Friends of The Library Membership Month Book Sale - Friends Preview Sale (w/ Annual Meeting)
cards.blurb: Hit the Friends Preview Sale before it opens to the public — dig through the tables for cheap paperbacks, cookbooks, and random gems you didn't know you needed.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and genuinely fun to browse if you like books.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 125 S Frances St, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a slow-drip Philz blend and decompress before dinner. The Pour Over is the move.
cards.why: Good way to wind down and ease into the evening.

cards.name: Spark60: Meditations for Mortals: The Liberating Art of Embracing Your Limits with Oliver Burkeman
cards.blurb: Oliver Burkeman wrote one of the more genuinely useful productivity books in years. This talk is about ditching the guilt of not doing it all and actually enjoying the finite time you have.
cards.why: Free talk that's actually worth your Thursday night.

cards.name: Urban Plates
cards.address: 300 W McKinley Ave Suite 100, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Urban Plates does made-from-scratch comfort food in a laid-back setting. Build a plate around whatever looks good on the board tonight.
cards.why: Solid food without a long wait or a big bill.
